title: OAuth client_secret stored plaintext in DB
description:
oauth_apps.client_secret is stored as the raw hex string (api.rs line 336, 345). DB read access (backup theft, SQL injection in another path, file access) directly yields all client secrets — enough to impersonate any registered OAuth app and exchange authorization codes for tokens.

Mitigations already in place: the secret is compared with subtle::ConstantTimeEq (api.rs line 787), so timing leaks are handled. But storage is not.

Fix: store SHA-256(client_secret) like oauth_tokens.token_hash. Return the raw secret only once at creation time (already done). Comparison becomes hash-then-lookup, same pattern as bearer tokens.
close_reason: Addressed by design doc (docs/design-key-storage.md) — implementation is a separate task
